HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?

Section: page_4.html [section_004] -[CONTENT]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Section: Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage? [section_005] -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Section: Does this plan meet Minimum Value Standards? [section_006] -[CONTENT]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

Section: About these Coverage Examples: [section_008] -[CONTENT]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE_TYPE]-> normal
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS_TYPE]-> normal
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION_TYPE]-> normal
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS_TYPE]-> normal
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION_TYPE]-> normal
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS_TYPE]-> normal
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION_TYPE]-> normal
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS_TYPE]-> normal
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION_TYPE]-> normal
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS_TYPE]-> normal
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION_TYPE]-> normal
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS_TYPE]-> normal
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION_TYPE]-> normal
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE_TYPE]-> normal
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.

Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.

HealthPlan -[HAS_SECTION]-> Mia’s Simple Fracture
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?

Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

HealthPlan -[HAS_SECTION]-> Mia’s Simple Fracture
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Summary of Benefits and Coverage: What this Plan Covers & What You Pay For Cover -[SUMMARY]-> Insurance Company 1: Plan Option 1 Coverage Period: 1/1/2017 – 12/31/2017 Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Footnote_footnote_001 -[CONTENT]-> All copayment and coinsurance costs shown in this chart are after your overall deductible has been met, if a deductible applies.
page_3.html -[SUMMARY]-> Services Your Plan Generally Does NOT Cover (Check your policy or plan document for more information and a list of any other excluded services.) Other Covered Services (Limitations may apply to these services. This isn't a complete list. Please see your plan document.)
Footnote_footnote_003 -[CONTENT]-> Your Rights to Continue Coverage: There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
page_4.html -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact:
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Minimum Value Standards -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

Coverage Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based
Footnote_footnote_009 -[CONTENT]-> Note: These numbers assume the patient does not participate in the plan’s wellness program. If you participate in the plan’s wellness program, you may be able to reduce your costs. For more information about the wellness program, please contact: [insert].

HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[CONTAINS_DOCUMENT_SECTION]-> Mia’s Simple Fracture
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?

Document Footnote footnote_003 -[DETAILS]-> Your Rights to Continue Coverage: There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
page_4.html -[DETAILS]-> Your Grievance and Appeals Rights: There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Does this plan provide Minimum Essential Coverage? -[DETAILS]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Does this plan meet Minimum Value Standards? -[DETAILS]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.

About these Coverage Examples: -[DETAILS]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. This EXAMPLE event includes services like: In this example, Peg would pay: This EXAMPLE event includes services like: In this example, Joe would pay:
Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice -[CONTENT]-> Note: These numbers assume the patient does not participate in the plan’s wellness program. If you participate in the plan’s wellness program, you may be able to reduce your costs. For more information about the wellness program, please contact: [insert].

HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage Compliance -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage Compliance -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage Compliance -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Continuation of Coverage Rights -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[OFFERS_WELLNESS_PROGRAM_SAVINGS]-> Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice
HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. Wellness Program Cost Reduction Notice -[CONTENT]-> Note: These numbers assume the patient does not participate in the plan’s wellness program. If you participate in the plan’s wellness program, you may be able to reduce your costs. For more information about the wellness program, please contact: [insert].

HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage. HealthPlan -[PLAN_TYPE]-> Coverage for: Family | Plan Type: PPO
Overall Deductible -[VALUE]-> $500/Individual or $1,000/family
Overall Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Generally, you must pay all of the costs from providers up to the deductible amount before this plan begins to pay. If you have other family members on the policy, they have to meet their own individual deductible until the overall family deductible amount has been met.
Overall Deductible -[QUESTION]-> What is the overall deductible?
Services Before Deductible -[VALUE]-> Yes. Preventive care and primary care services are covered before you meet your deductible.
Services Before Deductible -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan covers some items and services even if you haven’t yet met the annual deductible amount. But a copayment or coinsurance may apply. For example, this plan covers certain preventive services without cost-sharing and before you meet your deductible. See a list of covered preventive services at https://www.healthcare.gov/coverage/preventive-care-benefits/.
Services Before Deductible -[QUESTION]-> Are there services covered before you meet your deductible?
Service Specific Deductibles -[VALUE]-> Yes. $300 for prescription drug coverage and $300 for occupational therapy services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> You must pay all of the costs for these services up to the specific deductible amount before this plan begins to pay for these services.
Service Specific Deductibles -[QUESTION]-> Are there other deductibles for specific services?
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[VALUE]-> For network providers $2,500 individual / $5,000 family; for out-of-network providers $4,000 individual / $8,000 family
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> The out-of-pocket limit is the most you could pay in a year for covered services. If you have other family members in this plan, they have to meet their own out-of-pocket limits until the overall family out-of-pocket limit has been met.
Out-of-Pocket Limit -[QUESTION]-> What is the out-of-pocket limit for this plan?
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[VALUE]-> Copayments for certain services, premiums, balance-billed charges, and health care this plan doesn’t cover.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> Even though you pay these expenses, they don’t count toward the out-of-pocket limit.
Out-of-Pocket Exclusions -[QUESTION]-> What is not included in the out-of-pocket limit?
Network Provider Benefit -[VALUE]-> Yes. See www.[insert].com or call 1-800-[insert] for a list of network providers.
Network Provider Benefit -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan uses a provider network. You will pay less if you use a provider in the plan’s network. You will pay the most if you use an out-of-network provider, and you might receive a bill from a provider for the difference between the provider’s charge and what your plan pays (a balance bill). Be aware, your network provider might use an out-of-network provider for some services (such as lab work). Check with your provider before you get services.
Network Provider Benefit -[QUESTION]-> Will you pay less if you use a network provider?
Specialist Referral Requirement -[VALUE]-> Yes.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[WHY_IT_MATTERS]-> This plan will pay some or all of the costs to see a specialist for covered services but only if you have a referral before you see the specialist.
Specialist Referral Requirement -[QUESTION]-> Do you need a referral to see a specialist?
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you want to continue your coverage after it ends. The contact information for those agencies is: [insert State, HHS, DOL, and/or other applicable agency contact information]. Other options to continue coverage are available to you too, including buying individual insurance coverage through the Health Insurance Marketplace. For more information about the Marketplace, visit www.HealthCare.gov or call 1-800-318-2596.
Grievance and Appeals Rights -[SUMMARY]-> There are agencies that can help if you have a complaint against your plan for a denial of a claim. This complaint is called a grievance or appeal. For more information about your rights, look at the explanation of benefits you will receive for that medical claim. Your plan documents also provide complete information to submit a claim appeal or a grievance for any reason to your plan. For more information about your rights, this notice, or assistance, contact: [insert applicable contact information from instructions].
Minimum Essential Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If you don't have Minimum Essential Coverage for a month under this plan or under other coverage, you'll have to make a payment when you file your tax return unless you qualify for an exemption from the requirement that you have health coverage for that month.
Rights to Continue Coverage -[SUMMARY]-> Yes. If your plan doesn't meet the Minimum Value Standards, you may be eligible for a premium tax credit to help you pay for a plan through the Marketplace.
Language Assistance Services -[SUMMARY]-> [Spanish (Español): Para obtener asistencia en Español, llame al [insert telephone number].] [Tagalog (Tagalog): Kung kailangan ninyo ang tulong sa Tagalog tumawag sa [insert telephone number].] [Chinese (中文): 如果需要中文的帮助，请拨打这个号码 [insert telephone number].] [Navajo (Dine): Dinek'ehgo shika at'ohwol ninisingo, kwijjigo holne' [insert telephone number].] —To see examples of how this plan might cover costs for a sample medical situation, see the next section.—
Health Coverage Cost Examples -[SUMMARY]-> This is not a cost estimator. Treatments shown are just examples of how this plan might cover medical care. Your actual costs will be different depending on the actual care you receive, the prices your providers charge, and many other factors. Focus on the cost sharing amounts (deductibles, copayments and coinsurance) and excluded services under the plan. Use this information to compare the portion of costs you might pay under different health plans. Please note these coverage examples are based on self-only coverage.
